hook.text: English, Hindi, and Persian look nothing alike. But they share a common ancestor spoken 6,000 years ago.

buildup.text: A language family is a group of languages descended from a single ancestral language called a proto-language.

discovery.text: Indo-European is the largest family, but others include Sino-Tibetan, Niger-Congo, Austronesian, and many more.

twist.text: Some languages like Basque have no known relatives. They're called isolates — linguistic orphans with no family tree.

climax.text: Language families map human migration. Tracing words backward, linguists reconstruct paths walked millennia ago.

punchline.text: Languages carry the footprints of migration.

quiz.question: What is a language family?
options: Languages descended from a common ancestral language
